title: Herb drying and label routine
summary: Practical guidance for “Herb drying and label routine” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Herb drying and label routine” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop condition: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Reduced fallback: If “Herb drying and label routine” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a shelf-stable meal prepared with safe water and clean utensils.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Identify crop or animal needs, season, soil, water quality, exposure, labour, and the smallest useful trial. Record the result under “Food · Agriculture · Preservation” before continuing “Herb drying and label routine”.

Keep clean stock, seed, feed, harvest, compost, manure, chemicals, and diseased material separated and labelled. Recheck this point before closing “Herb drying and label routine” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Use observation rows or small test plots before changing the whole system. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Herb drying and label routine” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Record weather, watering, inputs, pests, disease signs, growth, yield, losses, and labour. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Herb drying and label routine”.

Save diverse healthy seed or breeding stock only from known sources and revise the next cycle from measured results. Include the outcome of “Herb drying and label routine” in the handover for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Herb drying and label routine” when growth, yield, water use, soil condition, pest pressure, disease, or animal welfare worsens after the change. Apply this boundary to “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop and reassess “Herb drying and label routine” when records cannot distinguish the trial from the control or the source of seed, feed, water, and inputs becomes uncertain. Apply this boundary to “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Herb drying and label routine” while temperature history, allergen status, flood contact, packaging integrity, or the origin of the food cannot be established. Apply this boundary to “Herb drying and label routine” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

This card is not authorization to taste questionable food to test it, hide spoilage with cooking, or serve an unknown ingredient to someone with a serious allergy.
